subject: Product Management
title: What Makes a Great PM
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: What Makes a Great PM.

hook.text: After decades of debate, the PM community still argues about what makes a truly great product manager.

buildup.text: Ben Horowitz says great PMs are 'the CEO of the product.' Marty Cagan says they solve problems worth solving.

discovery.text: Research points to three traits: intellectual curiosity, comfort with ambiguity, and relentless user focus.

twist.text: The rarest trait isn't analytical skill — it's the courage to kill a feature the team spent months building.

climax.text: Great PMs aren't defined by what they ship. They're defined by the clarity they bring to what matters.

punchline.text: Great PMs don't build more. They build what matters.

quiz.question: What rare trait distinguishes great PMs?
options: The courage to kill features the team spent months building
Being the best coder on the team
Always saying yes to stakeholder requests
